titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Rand Papers
titlestmt.author: ALSC staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 18, 2020 at 23:00

did.abstract: The papers consist of photographs; albums and scrapbooks; posters; resource files on design; correspondence, client, and subject files; slides; book jackets designed by Paul Rand; and booklets by and about Paul Rand.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Mrs. Paul Rand, 1999, 2000, 2001, 2012; transferred from Manuscripts and Archives to Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, 2014.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Rand Papers, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Paul Rand was born in Brooklyn, New York, in 1914. He was a professor of graphic design at Yale University from 1956 until his retirement in 1985. He achieved the title of professor emeritus in 1993. Rand is known for his work in the field of trademark design. He was the art director for numerous publications and consultant to many corporations and advertising agencies. Rand died on November 26, 1996, in Norwalk, Connecticut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of photographs; albums and scrapbooks; posters; resource files on design; correspondence, client, and subject files; slides; book jackets designed by Paul Rand; and booklets by and about Paul Rand.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in six series: I. Photographs. II. Albums and Scrapbooks. III. Resource File on Design. IV. Book Jackets Designed by Paul Rand. V. Booklets by and about Paul Rand. VI. Mechanicals and Samples of Paul Rand's Work., and four additional accessions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of photographic negatives and prints of work produced primarily by Paul Rand. Extensive duplication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of scrapbook-type binders containing examples of Rand's work, articles about his career and work and awards he received.. The binder labeled "Scrap" contains examples not produced by Rand. Some binders contain numerous empty pages.

did.unittitle: Publicity albums (volumes 12-16)(volume 12 continued in folder 2 of 2)
"Scrap" [design and advertising examples; includes colonial scrip]
Forum, Disney, Kaiser Frazer, Knopf, Container Corp., Shur-Edge, Esquire, Rain Fair, 20th Century Fox, Olivetti, Maix

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of articles, clippings, exhibition catalogs, occasional correspondence with designers and others, and examples of works by other designers. Includes files on individuals, corporations, topics, genres, and geographic areas.

did.unittitle: Book jackets designed by Paul Rand
Booklets by and about Paul Rand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of presentation booklets containing Rand proposals to organizations for their design work, booklets produced by Rand containing his thoughts on design, and two pieces containing an interview of and quotes from Rand.

did.unittitle: "Observations on Intuition" in Analysis and Intuition
Mechanicals and samples of Paul Rand's work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of mechanicals, proofs, and completed works. Should be integrated logically into job bags or other series when they arrive. This material was transferred in four packages (numbered 3, 4, 5, and 7 of 7) in which it had been placed following the close of an exhibit.

did.unittitle: Sparkle and Spin, by Paul and Ann Rand
Little 1, by Paul and Ann Rand
No Way Out - 24 sheet movie poster (color image of poster included)
Bab-o No Sink Smog - 24 sheet advertising poster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bab-O was a bathroom and kitchen cleaning product.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Published as a salute to the people of France by the makers of Lee Hats, whose privilege it was to broadcast the programs on which Drew Pearson fostered and initiated the Friendship Trains..."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of 35 mm color photographic slides of Paul Rand's projects.

The addition contained identifying labels for the following businesses/subject headings for which there were no associated slides: Adstar, Apparel arts, Container Corp., Coronet, El Producto – Lovera, Gentry, PDR, Stafford, Target, Tipton Lakes, and U.S. Department of the Interior. The labels have been removed, but it is possible that images for these categories may be found in the general or unidentified slides.
